title: IndiGo Goes Long-Haul: Leased 787s Open Amsterdam and Manchester
dek: India's largest low-cost carrier stretches its all-narrow-body model into Europe with damp-leased Boeing 787-9s, opening bookings for non-stop Mumbai service.
body:
IndiGo, long the standard-bearer for India's single-aisle, point-to-point model, took a decisive step into the long-haul arena in mid-2025, opening sales for non-stop flights from Mumbai to Amsterdam and Manchester. Rather than wait for its own wide-bodies, the carrier moved early using Boeing 787-9 Dreamliners damp-leased from Norse Atlantic Airways, with the European services slated to begin in July. The move marks a notable widening of ambition for an airline whose growth was built almost entirely on the Airbus A320 family.

A bridge strategy before the A350 arrives

The damp-lease arrangement, in which Norse supplies aircraft and cockpit crew while IndiGo handles commercial operations and cabin service, lets the airline test premium-light long-haul demand without committing its own metal or pilots immediately. It is best understood as a bridge to the airline's firm Airbus A350-900 order, which it has been steadily building out as the backbone of a future wide-body fleet.

Mumbai and Delhi give IndiGo dense origin-and-destination markets and meaningful Europe-bound traffic, reducing reliance on connecting feed to fill long-haul cabins. Amsterdam and Manchester also offer onward network potential without going head-to-head with every legacy hub on day one.

Why the timing matters

India's outbound travel market has expanded quickly, and rivals including a reconstituted Air India have signalled aggressive international intentions. By entering Europe now, IndiGo aims to establish brand presence and slot positions ahead of its own wide-body deliveries.

  • Aircraft: Boeing 787-9, damp-leased from Norse Atlantic
  • Initial European points: Amsterdam (AMS) and Manchester (MAN)
  • Primary Indian gateway: Mumbai (BOM)
  • Longer-term fleet plan: firm Airbus A350-900 order

Executing a credible long-haul product, from cabin comfort to reliability, will be the real test as the leased Dreamliners enter service.
heroImageAlt: A Boeing 787-9 Dreamliner climbing after takeoff against a clear sky on a long-haul departure
metaTitle: IndiGo Long-Haul Debut: 787s to Amsterdam, Manchester
metaDescription: IndiGo enters long-haul flying with damp-leased Boeing 787-9s to Amsterdam and Manchester from Mumbai, a bridge to its firm Airbus A350-900 order.
